title: Build contexts get mixed up
body:
### Issue Description

In some cases, files end up in a wrong image when building multiple services via compose.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ tree --charset=ascii
.
|-- apps
|   |-- bar
|   |   |-- Dockerfile
|   |   `-- data
|   `-- foo
|       |-- Dockerfile
|       `-- data
`-- compose.yaml

4 directories, 5 files
```
```
$ cat apps/foo/Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1 AS build

RUN mkdir /out
RUN --mount=source=./data,target=data \
    cp data /out


FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1

COPY --from=build /out /out

ENTRYPOINT ["cat", "/out/data"]
```

```
$ cat apps/bar/Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1 AS build

RUN mkdir /out
RUN --mount=source=./data,target=data \
    cp data /out


FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1

COPY --from=build /out /out

ENTRYPOINT ["cat", "/out/data"]
```

```
$ cat apps/foo/data
DATA: foo
```

```
$ cat apps/bar/data
DATA: bar
```

```
$ cat compose.yaml
services:
  foo:
    build:
      context: ./apps/foo
  bar:
    build:
      context: ./apps/bar
```

### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman compose up --build --force-recreate --remove 2>&1 | grep DATA
[bar] | DATA: bar
[foo] | DATA: foo

$ podman compose up --build --force-recreate --remove 2>&1 | grep DATA
[bar] | DATA: bar
[foo] | DATA: bar

$ podman compose up --build --force-recreate --remove 2>&1 | grep DATA
[bar] | DATA: bar
[foo] | DATA: bar

$ podman compose down
>>>> Executing external compose provider "/nix/store/ppwj4hj2rkxhrvg95mwbb34walh3q48j-podman-compose-1.6.0/bin/podman-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

test_foo_1
test_bar_1
test_bar_1
test_foo_1
326bbf15e58a8b05908274611c5869ad209d3fa236b5b213ded45a272d328ac2
test_default

$ podman system prune -f 2>&1 >/dev/null

$ podman compose up --build --force-recreate --remove 2>&1 | grep DATA
[foo] | DATA: foo
[bar] | DATA: bar

$ podman compose up --build --force-recreate --remove 2>&1 | grep DATA
[foo] | DATA: foo
[bar] | DATA: foo

$ podman compose up --build --force-recreate --remove 2>&1 | grep DATA
[foo] | DATA: foo
[bar] | DATA: foo
```

### Describe the results you expected

Files should be copied from the right context.

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman info

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.44.0
  cdiSpecDirs:

    version: 'conmon version 2.2.1, commit: c8cc2c4db27531bd4e084ce7857f73cd21ee639d'
  cpuUtilization:

    - container_id: 0

    - container_id: 0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: On second thought, I guess this should be submitted to https://github.com/containers/podman-compose.

Looks like this is a podman issue after all. I can reproduce it without compose:

```
$ podman build -t foo apps/foo && podman run --rm foo
[1/2] STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1 AS build
[1/2] STEP 2/3: RUN mkdir /out
--> 153c2bb1f6b5
[1/2] STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=source=./data,target=data     cp data /out
--> 4f7cb4a3831c
[2/2] STEP 1/4: FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1
[2/2] STEP 2/4: COPY --from=build /out /out
--> 258ff80a515d
[2/2] STEP 3/4: RUN true
--> aad88599c0e3
[2/2] STEP 4/4: ENTRYPOINT ["cat", "/out/data"]
[2/2] COMMIT foo
--> 42df2949f348
Successfully tagged localhost/foo:latest
42df2949f348005b4a26d49805c438f98d5ef3f1fe6d69a0733c3689286b8133
DATA: foo
```

```
$ podman build -t bar apps/bar && podman run --rm bar
[1/2] STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1 AS build
[1/2] STEP 2/3: RUN mkdir /out
--> Using cache 153c2bb1f6b56ec8d9ce0c678dafa70a26c331b816d7b8b9a5ad057253007e78
--> 153c2bb1f6b5
[1/2] STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=source=./data,target=data     cp data /out
--> Using cache 4f7cb4a3831cb562902ce35b80773e0d64a818df3823c3d83cad62e1bef40ced
--> 4f7cb4a3831c
[2/2] STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/alpine:3.24.1
[2/2] STEP 2/3: COPY --from=build /out /out
--> Using cache 258ff80a515dedde12b3ac35dcdddf3d96ac7440c80cc63a97a751499bb023d3
--> 258ff80a515d
[2/2] STEP 3/3: ENTRYPOINT ["cat", "/out/data"]
[2/2] COMMIT bar
--> 2b9da5c8742c
Successfully tagged localhost/bar:latest
2b9da5c8742c5eb60a181770fe526a17dc87343bad20560be8785b440f5f31a0
DATA: foo
```

So it looks like the second build uses the cached `RUN --mount=source=./data` even though it is a different file.
I can work on it. Can it be assigned to me?

Hey @dmytro-GL and @danishprakash, so I dug into this and good news — it's not actually a podman bug, it's coming from buildah (vendored in here).

Here's what's going on: when you write `RUN --mount=source=./data,target=data` without explicitly adding `type=bind` (which is completely normal — bind is the default when you don't say otherwise), there's a spot in buildah's caching code that figures out the mount type for cache-key purposes, and it doesn't apply that same "no type = bind" default. So it ends up treating your mount as "not a bind mount" and skips hashing the file's contents — which is exactly the check that's supposed to catch cases like `foo` and `bar` having different `data` files. Since that checksum never happens, the two builds end up with identical cache keys even though the underlying file is different, and that's the mixup you're seeing.

I reproduced it locally and confirmed a fix (just making that one default match what the rest of buildah already does). Since the actual bug lives upstream, I'm going to open a PR against buildah directly rather than patching it here — I'll follow up on this issue with the buildah PR link, and again with the podman-side vendor bump once it's merged and released. Thanks for the clear repro, by the way — made this way easier to track down.
